rules.title: Adjectival Pronouns - Overview
rules.text: Adjectival pronouns in Slovak decline like adjectives through all 7 cases and agree with nouns in gender (masculine, feminine, neuter), number (singular, plural), and case. They include demonstrative (this, that), possessive (my, your, his, her), interrogative (which, whose), relative (which, whose), and possessive reflexive (one's own) pronouns. Unlike substantive pronouns, these modify nouns rather than replace them.
examples: Demonstrative: tento dom (this house), tá žena (that woman)
Possessive: môj brat (my brother), tvoja sestra (your sister)
Interrogative: ktorý muž? (which man?), čí dom? (whose house?)
Relative: muž, ktorý pracuje (the man who works)
Reflexive possessive: svoj dom (one's own house)

rules.title: Demonstrative Pronouns - 'tento/táto/toto' (this)
rules.text: The demonstrative pronoun 'tento' (this) is used for objects close to the speaker. It declines like a hard-stem adjective with the prefix 'ten-/tá-/to-'.
examples: Nominative: Tento muž je dobrý - This man is good
Genitive: Cena tohto domu - The price of this house
Dative: Dám to tomuto mužovi - I'll give it to this man
Accusative: Vidím tohto muža - I see this man
Locative: V tomto dome - In this house
Instrumental: S týmto mužom - With this man

rules.title: Demonstrative Pronouns - 'ten/tá/to' (that)
rules.text: The demonstrative pronoun 'ten' (that) is used for objects farther from the speaker or previously mentioned. It's the most common demonstrative.
examples: Nominative: Ten muž je môj priateľ - That man is my friend
Genitive: Bez toho muža - Without that man
Dative: K tomu domu - To that house
Accusative: Vidím toho muža - I see that man
Locative: V tom dome - In that house
Instrumental: S tým mužom - With that man

rules.title: Demonstrative Pronouns - 'tamten/tamtá/tamto' (that over there)
rules.text: The demonstrative pronoun 'tamten' (that over there) emphasizes distance. It combines 'tam' (there) with 'ten' and declines similarly.
examples: Nominative: Tamten dom je veľký - That house over there is big
Genitive: Majiteľ tamtoho domu - The owner of that house over there
Dative: Idem k tamtomu domu - I'm going to that house over there
Accusative: Vidíš tamten dom? - Do you see that house over there?
Locative: V tamtom dome - In that house over there
Instrumental: S tamtým mužom - With that man over there

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns - 'môj/moja/moje' (my, mine)
rules.text: The possessive pronoun 'môj' (my, mine) shows ownership by the first person singular. It declines like a soft-stem adjective.
examples: Nominative: Môj brat je tu - My brother is here
Genitive: Dom môjho brata - My brother's house
Dative: Dám to môjmu bratovi - I'll give it to my brother
Accusative: Vidím môjho brata - I see my brother
Locative: V mojom dome - In my house
Instrumental: S mojím bratom - With my brother

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns - 'tvoj/tvoja/tvoje' (your, yours)
rules.text: The possessive pronoun 'tvoj' (your, yours - singular informal) shows ownership by the second person singular. Declines identically to 'môj'.
examples: Nominative: Tvoj dom je pekný - Your house is nice
Genitive: Farba tvojho domu - The color of your house
Dative: K tvojmu domu - To your house
Accusative: Vidím tvoj dom - I see your house
Locative: Vo tvojom dome - In your house
Instrumental: S tvojím bratom - With your brother

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns - 'náš/naša/naše' (our, ours)
rules.text: The possessive pronoun 'náš' (our, ours) shows ownership by the first person plural. It declines like a soft-stem adjective.
examples: Nominative: Náš dom je veľký - Our house is big
Genitive: Záhrada nášho domu - The garden of our house
Dative: K nášmu domu - To our house
Accusative: Vidíš náš dom? - Do you see our house?
Locative: V našom dome - In our house
Instrumental: S naším otcom - With our father

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns - 'váš/vaša/vaše' (your, yours - plural/formal)
rules.text: The possessive pronoun 'váš' (your, yours - plural or formal) shows ownership by the second person plural or formal singular. Declines like 'náš'.
examples: Nominative: Váš dom je krásny - Your house is beautiful
Genitive: Strecha vášho domu - The roof of your house
Dative: K vášmu domu - To your house
Accusative: Obdivujem váš dom - I admire your house
Locative: Vo vašom dome - In your house
Instrumental: S vaším synom - With your son

rules.title: Possessive Reflexive Pronoun - 'svoj/svoja/svoje' (one's own)
rules.text: The reflexive possessive pronoun 'svoj' (one's own) refers back to the subject of the sentence. It's used for all persons and numbers. Declines like 'môj'.
examples: Vidím svoj dom - I see my (own) house
Vidíš svoj dom - You see your (own) house
Vidí svoj dom - He/she sees his/her (own) house
Hovorím o svojom bratovi - I'm talking about my (own) brother
Idem so svojím otcom - I'm going with my (own) father
Milujú svoje deti - They love their (own) children

rules.title: Third Person Possessive - 'jeho/jej/ich' (his/her/their)
rules.text: Third person possessive forms are invariable (don't decline). 'Jeho' (his, its), 'jej' (her), 'ich' (their) remain unchanged in all cases.
examples: Nominative: Jeho brat je tu - His brother is here
Genitive: Dom jeho brata - His brother's house
Dative: Dám to jeho bratovi - I'll give it to his brother
Jej sestra je pekná - Her sister is pretty
Ich rodičia sú milí - Their parents are nice
Hovorím o jeho práci - I'm talking about his work

rules.title: Interrogative Pronoun - 'ktorý/ktorá/ktoré' (which, what)
rules.text: The interrogative pronoun 'ktorý' (which, what) asks about selection from a group. It declines like a hard-stem adjective.
examples: Nominative: Ktorý muž? - Which man?
Genitive: Ktorého muža vidíš? - Which man do you see?
Dative: Ktorému mužovi to dáš? - To which man will you give it?
Accusative: Ktorú knihu chceš? - Which book do you want?
Locative: V ktorom dome bývate? - In which house do you live?
Instrumental: S ktorým priateľom ideš? - With which friend are you going?

rules.title: Interrogative Pronoun - 'čí/čia/čie' (whose)
rules.text: The interrogative pronoun 'čí' (whose) asks about possession. It declines like a soft-stem adjective.
examples: Nominative: Čí je to dom? - Whose house is it?
Genitive: Čieho brata vidíš? - Whose brother do you see?
Dative: Čiemu dieťaťu to dáš? - To whose child will you give it?
Accusative: Čiu knihu čítaš? - Whose book are you reading?
Locative: V čom dome bývate? - In whose house do you live?
Instrumental: S čím bratom hovoríš? - With whose brother are you talking?

rules.title: Relative Pronoun - 'ktorý/ktorá/ktoré' (who, which, that)
rules.text: The relative pronoun 'ktorý' (who, which, that) introduces relative clauses. It declines identically to the interrogative 'ktorý' and agrees with its antecedent.
examples: Nominative: Muž, ktorý pracuje - The man who works
Genitive: Muž, ktorého poznám - The man whom I know
Dative: Muž, ktorému som dal knihu - The man to whom I gave a book
Accusative: Žena, ktorú vidím - The woman whom I see
Locative: Dom, v ktorom bývam - The house in which I live
Instrumental: Priateľ, s ktorým idem - The friend with whom I go

rules.title: Relative Pronoun - 'čí/čia/čie' (whose)
rules.text: The relative pronoun 'čí' (whose) introduces relative clauses showing possession. It declines like the interrogative 'čí'.
examples: Muž, čí dom je veľký - The man whose house is big
Žena, čieho syna poznám - The woman whose son I know
Priateľ, čiemu bratovi som pomohol - The friend to whose brother I helped
Dieťa, čiu matku vidím - The child whose mother I see
Rodina, v čom dome bývame - The family in whose house we live
Človek, s čím synom pracujem - The person with whose son I work

rules.title: Demonstrative Pronouns - Summary Table
rules.text: Complete declension pattern summary for demonstrative pronouns. Pattern applies to tento/ten/tamten with appropriate stem.

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns - Summary Table (môj/tvoj/svoj pattern)
rules.text: Complete declension pattern for possessive pronouns môj, tvoj, svoj (and similarly náš, váš with different stems).

rules.title: Interrogative/Relative Pronouns - Summary Table (ktorý pattern)
rules.text: Complete declension pattern for interrogative and relative pronoun 'ktorý' (which, who, that).

rules.title: Interrogative/Relative Pronouns - Summary Table (čí pattern)
rules.text: Complete declension pattern for interrogative and relative pronoun 'čí' (whose).
